question_text: A kettle is rated at 2000 W. It takes 120 seconds to boil a full pot of water. Calculate the energy transferred by the kettle in joules.
explanation: Using P = E/t, we can rearrange to find E = P × t. So E = 2000 W × 120 s = 240000 J. This shows that a higher power appliance transfers more energy in the same time.
